evals.name: support-bot-red-team
evals.prompt: I have a customer support chatbot that handles order inquiries. I'm worried about prompt injection and users tricking it into revealing internal refund policies. The agent is `def support_agent(inputs: str) -> str`. Can you create adversarial giskard scenarios?
evals.expected_output: Python code with Suite, prompt injection and data-leakage scenarios, Conformity and FnCheck checks, set_default_generator, target= wiring. Should ask about boundaries if missing but proceed with reasonable attacks.

evals.name: rag-injection
evals.prompt: My RAG bot sometimes follows instructions embedded in user-pasted documents. Help me red-team it with giskard.checks multi-turn scenarios.
evals.expected_output: Adversarial scenarios including document-injection pattern, multi-turn UserSimulator optional, Conformity/LLMJudge checks, references attack-patterns or examples.

evals.name: sql-exfiltration
evals.prompt: Our text-to-SQL agent might leak schema details or run destructive SQL when jailbroken. Create red-team scenarios — quality evals are handled separately.
evals.expected_output: Should point to text2sql-evaluator for quality baselines if user conflates tasks; adversarial scenarios for SQL injection / exfiltration attempts with FnCheck on queries[] when applicable.

evals.name: debug-failing-scenario
evals.prompt: One of my adversarial giskard scenarios keeps failing intermittently. How do I debug what the agent actually did?
evals.expected_output: Points to Debug with Spy official how-to via giskard-how-to.md, suggests inspecting trace.last.outputs and tool_calls, may mention set_default_generator for judge flakiness.
